skill.name: issue-triage
skill.description: Guides AI agents through GitHub issue diagnosis, classification, decision support, and professional response drafting.
skill.summary: Triage GitHub issues with structured root cause analysis and response drafting.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography and obfuscation are false positives: SKILL.md line 3 is front matter description text, not cryptographic code, and the file is readable Markdown. Backtick findings at lines 108 and 119 are Markdown fence delimiters, while line 23 and line 131 describe legitimate GitHub CLI commands for issue triage. The skill is publishable with a medium risk rating because it can lead an agent to run external GitHub commands, including a state-changing comment action after user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: External GitHub CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to use GitHub CLI commands to view issue details and post an issue comment. This is legitimate for issue triage, but it can read repository issue data and perform a state-changing action if executed with an authenticated GitHub session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented in the workflow and match the skill purpose. Risk is moderate because posting a comment changes remote GitHub state, but the file also requires user confirmation before publication.

low_findings.title: False Positive Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but the relevant lines are Markdown template delimiters and inline command formatting. No executable script or dynamic command construction is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside a Markdown fenced reply template. There is no Ruby code, shell script, or execution context at these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm, but that line is the skill description in front matter. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or encryption workflow appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains natural language metadata only. The finding does not correspond to any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive Obfuscation Heuristic
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy at SKILL.md line 1, but the file is readable Markdown with Chinese prose and normal front matter. No encoded payload, binary content, or hidden instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found a plain text Markdown skill. The entropy signal is likely caused by multilingual text rather than obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes a GitHub CLI comment step. This pattern is not malicious, but agents should only run it after the user approves the final reply.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The state-changing action is directly described in the publish step. The workflow includes confirmation requirements, which reduces but does not remove risk.

content.value_statement: Maintainers often need to diagnose issues before giving a public answer. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through diagnosis, classification, decision support, and response drafting.
actual_capabilities: Reads a provided GitHub issue link or repository context.
Extracts environment details, reproduction steps, expected behavior, and actual behavior.
Guides code investigation from user-facing entry points through implementation paths.
Classifies issues as bugs, architecture limits, feature requests, or usage questions.
Drafts structured, professional GitHub issue replies after analysis.
Reminds the agent to request user approval before posting a reply.
limitations: Requires repository access and enough issue detail to diagnose the problem.
Does not guarantee a correct root cause without readable code and reproduction context.
May use GitHub CLI commands when available and authorized by the user.
Does not automatically create code fixes or test plans.

use_cases.title: Analyze a Bug Report
use_cases.description: Trace the reported behavior through code and identify whether it is a real defect.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Request Reply
use_cases.description: Explain scope, cost, priority, and possible alternatives before accepting or deferring a request.

use_cases.title: Improve Support Responses
use_cases.description: Turn an unclear issue into a respectful reply with context, next steps, and workaround guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Issue Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with one GitHub issue link.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-triage to analyze this GitHub issue: [issue link]. Summarize the report, identify missing information, and wait for my confirmation before deeper diagnosis.

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Diagnose an issue against a local repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-triage on this issue: [issue link]. Follow the relevant code path, cite files and line numbers, and explain the most likely root cause.

prompt_templates.title: Decision Recommendation
prompt_templates.scenario: Decide whether to fix, defer, or close.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-triage to classify this issue and recommend whether to fix it now. Include scope, affected modules, test requirements, and user workaround options.

prompt_templates.title: Publish-Ready Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Use issue-triage to draft a GitHub issue reply based on our confirmed diagnosis and decision. Do not post it until I explicitly approve the final text.

output_examples.input: A user reports that export fails only when a project has nested folders.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes the issue, traces the export path, classifies the report as a bug or design gap, and drafts a maintainer reply.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a new integration that requires a new backend module.
output_examples.output: The skill separates current behavior from requested capability, estimates implementation scope, and suggests a clear response with alternatives.

output_examples.input: A user posts an issue with unclear reproduction steps.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies missing environment details and drafts a concise follow-up question before making a technical conclusion.

best_practices: Read the full issue before starting diagnosis.
Confirm classification and decision points with the user.
Review any GitHub CLI command before running it.
anti_patterns: Do not classify an issue from the title alone.
Do not post a GitHub comment without explicit user approval.
Do not promise a fix when testing conditions are missing.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps analyze GitHub issues, find root causes, classify requests, and draft professional maintainer replies.

faq.question: Does it automatically post replies?
faq.answer: No. The workflow requires user confirmation before a reply is published to GitHub.

faq.question: Can it work without GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use provided issue text or web access when available, but GitHub CLI can improve workflow speed.

faq.question: Does it fix code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on triage, diagnosis, decision support, and reply drafting.

faq.question: What issue types does it support?
faq.answer: It supports bug reports, architecture limits, feature requests, and usage questions.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill references external GitHub CLI commands, including a command that can post a comment after approval.
